svg.text: Scenarios 1–4 above — single-shot, self-repair, and cache-fronted routing.
A second prompt tripping a BannedPhraseRule is
blocked here — no arrow reaches ProviderGatewayKit,
and nothing is metered for it.
Scenario 8 — TraceKit nested tracing + eval gate
RetrievalKit has no compile-time dependency on ProviderGatewayKit — retrieveContextBlock(query:)'s
output is plain text, prepended to the prompt exactly as a host app would do it itself.
typed RAGAnswer, grounded in rendered prompt
after routing, this demo also calls
PromptTemplateKit has no compile-time dependency on ProviderGatewayKit — only the
render(_:variables:mode:) output (plain text) is ever handed to LLMSession.send(_:), the same seam every sibling kit uses.
CircuitBreaker's default failureThreshold is 3 consecutive failures, so two failures followed by a
success never trips it — the same LLMSession is safely retried, not rebuilt, across all three attempts.
The pinned system prompt (LLMSession's own systemPrompt) survives compaction untouched — only the
unpinned conversation turns are dropped, shrunk, or summarized. ContextCompactionKit has no compile-time
dependency on ProviderGatewayKit — the compacted messages are joined into plain text and handed to the
next session.send(_:) call, the same seam every sibling kit in this ecosystem uses.
All twelve packages resolved from their real, tagged 1.0.0 releases — see README for the full flow.
